title: PROP-046 amendment-001 — concrete implementation patch set (2 writer-site diffs + regression check, all 3 Q-OP answers applied verbatim)
operator_decisions_applied: All 3 Q-OP answers from DIRECTIVE-20260517-004.operator_decisions are accepted as-written. Q1 (field name): use `target_mode` — matches existing tinker.md dispatcher vocabulary; single field-name convention across operator-directive and attention-inbox consumers; no new schema vocabulary required. Q2 (PROP029-ORPHAN lane): `tinker` bare, no sub-mode — let tinker's Mode 1/2/3/4 dispatcher select based on directive content; the current orphan-batch is a one-time spike, not a recurring pattern that warrants a dedicated tinker sub-mode. Q3 (resolved field): keep `resolved: false` alongside `status: 'pending'` — additive change is safer than a schema change; analyst Mode 2b's filter logic in monitor/prompts/analyst.md line 108-112 reads `status === 'pending'` (canonical) and the `resolved` field is the redundant-but-safe sibling that downstream consumers (e.g., legacy archive-on-resolve patterns) may still rely on. Dropping `resolved` could create silent classification gaps. STALE-ASSESS items get `target_mode: 'analyst-mode-1b'`; PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED items get `target_mode: 'tinker'`.
scope_disclaimer: IMPLEMENTATION AMENDMENT ONLY. This file is a comprehensive patch set for operator + cowork-claude to apply in a single coordinated session. Tinker does NOT self-apply any patch in this amendment per directive scope_authority guardrail ('Tinker does NOT self-apply.'). No new design questions are raised here per directive out_of_scope_for_tinker[1] ('Do NOT raise additional design questions. The 3 Q-OPs are decided.'). Scope is held to the two writer sites named in PROP-046 (decider-intake.md Step 1a stale-skip writer + analyst-mode1-expansions.md PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED writer) per directive out_of_scope_for_tinker[2].
relationship_to_parent: Sibling additive document. PROP-046 status remains 'design-pending-operator-review' until operator-promotion to 'approved-for-implementation' after this amendment lands. This amendment supplies the concrete old_string/new_string content that PROP-046.phase_1_design enumerated abstractly. Field-name decision (target_mode rather than the recommended_lane that PROP-046 design proposed) is captured in this amendment's patches; PROP-046's body is NOT mutated.
operator_preferences_honored: Single comprehensive amendment, not multiple round trips — both writer-site patches bundled here.
Every prompt edit given as exact old_string/new_string pair — PROP-014-amendment-001 format with verbatim multi-line strings ready for Edit-tool use.
Regression check section mandatory — see regression_check below; confirms both writer sites produce valid attention-inbox JSON post-patch (analyst Mode 2b dispatcher reads cleanly).
Operator + cowork-claude apply in single session after amendment authored — both patches are independent, can be applied in any order, both reversible by single-line removal.
summary_of_changes: Two markdown-file edits, one writer-block each. Total 6 line-additions across 2 files. No code logic changes anywhere. The two writers (decider-intake.md Step 1a stale-skip; analyst-mode1-expansions.md PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED) currently emit `resolved: false` with NO `status` field — invisible to Mode 2b's `status === 'pending'` filter. Each patch inserts `status: 'pending'` (canonical PROP-022 schema) + `target_mode: '<lane>'` (advisory routing per Q1 operator decision) immediately before the existing `resolved: false` line. The `resolved: false` line is preserved unchanged per Q3 operator decision (backward-compat with legacy consumers that may read it). Each patch's old_string is a 3-line block uniquely matching exactly once in the target file (verified by grep at authoring time). After application, future emits from both writer paths will be visible to Mode 2b dispatcher and will carry an explicit lane label for any future dispatcher-routing logic.

patches.writer_site: Step 1a (PROP-028 Mech A stale-assessment check, fenced ```javascript block, lines ~240-253)
patches.purpose: Add `status: 'pending'` (canonical PROP-022 schema field) and `target_mode: 'analyst-mode-1b'` (advisory routing hint per Q1+Q2 decisions) to the appendAttentionInbox object literal. STALE-ASSESS items track prediction-reassessment work whose next-consumer is analyst Mode 1b (prediction writeups); the lane label directly names that consumer. The `resolved: false` line is preserved unchanged per Q3 decision.
patches.new_string:
action_recommended: 'Re-run analyst if fresh assessment needed; otherwise leave as-is.',
         created_by: 'decider-stale-skip',
         created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
         status: 'pending',
         target_mode: 'analyst-mode-1b',
         resolved: false
       });
patches.uniqueness_verification: grep -c "created_by: 'decider-stale-skip'" monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md → 1 (verified 2026-05-17). The 3-line block ending in `resolved: false\n       });` is unique within this file.
patches.post_application_check: grep -n 'target_mode' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md → should return exactly one line containing `target_mode: 'analyst-mode-1b',` within ~5 lines of `created_by: 'decider-stale-skip'`.

patches.writer_site: Check for Orphaned Issues section, fenced ```json block illustrating the PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED item shape, lines ~68-79
patches.purpose: Add `status: 'pending'` (canonical PROP-022 schema field) and `target_mode: 'tinker'` (advisory routing hint per Q1+Q2 decisions) to the JSON example. PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED items are pipeline-handoff diagnostics whose next-consumer is tinker (investigates the decider tracker-write decoupling root-cause). Per Q2 operator decision, the lane is bare `tinker` with no sub-mode — tinker's Mode 1-4 dispatcher selects based on directive content; the orphan-batch is a one-time spike, not a recurring pattern that warrants a dedicated tinker sub-mode. The `resolved: false` line is preserved unchanged per Q3 decision.

"action_taken_this_run": "<N> orphans grouped into <M> EXP entries and worked normally; tracker decoupling root-cause TBD.",

  "target_mode": "tinker",

}
patches.uniqueness_verification: grep -c "action_taken_this_run" monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode1-expansions.md → 1 (verified 2026-05-17). The 3-line block ending in `"resolved": false\n}` is unique within this file (only one PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED writer exists).
patches.post_application_check: grep -n 'target_mode' monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode1-expansions.md → should return exactly one line containing `"target_mode": "tinker"` within ~5 lines of `PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED`.

regression_check.purpose_one_line: Walk the amendment for: (a) does it disrupt analyst Mode 2b dispatcher's current filter logic? (b) does it conflict with any other inbox writer's schema? (c) confirm the existing 6 STALE-ASSESS items (already operator-direct-fixed in commit 3688bbe with schema_fix_at field) won't be re-touched. — per DIRECTIVE-20260517-004 tasks_for_tinker[1].

check_a_analyst_mode2b_dispatcher_unchan.question: Does adding `status: 'pending'` + `target_mode: '<lane>'` disrupt analyst Mode 2b's current filter logic?
check_a_analyst_mode2b_dispatcher_unchan.current_dispatcher_logic_pasted:
From monitor/prompts/analyst.md line 156:
  `const p = arr.filter(x => x.status === 'pending');`
Mode 2b filters by `status === 'pending'` ONLY. The `target_mode` field is NEW and is not read by any current consumer.
check_a_analyst_mode2b_dispatcher_unchan.reasoning: Mode 2b filter currently misses both writers' items because they emit `resolved: false` with NO `status` field — `x.status === 'pending'` evaluates to `false` and the items are silently skipped. After patches, both writers will emit `status: 'pending'`, restoring Mode 2b visibility (the desired fix). The added `target_mode` field is purely advisory metadata — Mode 2b's current logic does NOT branch on `target_mode`, so adding it is a no-op for current dispatcher behavior. A future dispatcher upgrade (out-of-scope per PROP-046.phase_2_optional_followup) could route by `target_mode` value, but until then the field is inert and harmless.
check_a_analyst_mode2b_dispatcher_unchan.supporting_grep: grep -rn 'target_mode' monitor/prompts/ → returns 3 matches, all in monitor/prompts/tinker.md operator-directive dispatcher (line 44, 50, 54). Zero matches in analyst.md, decider.md, curmudgeon.md, or any reference file. Confirmed: no current consumer reads `target_mode` from attention-inbox items, so adding the field cannot break any consumer.

check_b_no_conflict_with_other_inbox_wri.question: Does this conflict with any other attention-inbox writer's schema?
check_b_no_conflict_with_other_inbox_wri.method: grep -rn 'appendAttentionInbox\|attention-inbox' monitor/prompts/ → returned 6 files at PROP-046 authoring time. Re-verified at amendment authoring:

writers_inventory.section: Step A — End-of-Run Analyst Attention Inbox (lines ~376-389)
writers_inventory.current_schema: Already emits `status: 'pending'` + `resolved: false`. Does NOT currently emit `target_mode`.
writers_inventory.impact_of_this_patch: NO IMPACT. This writer is not in PROP-046 scope. Future cleanup PROP (PROP-046.phase_2_optional_followup) could add `target_mode` here too for symmetric shape, but the current patch leaves it untouched. Adding `target_mode` to two writers but not the third does NOT create a conflict because no consumer reads the field today — it is advisory-only metadata. The three writers will produce three slightly-different shapes (Step A: status + resolved; new STALE-ASSESS: status + target_mode + resolved; new PROP029-ORPHAN: status + target_mode + resolved), but the only consumer that branches at all (Mode 2b) reads `status` only, which is uniform across all three.

writers_inventory.current_schema: Emits `resolved: false`, no `status`. THIS PATCH SITE.
writers_inventory.impact_of_this_patch: Patch 1 applied. Post-patch: status + target_mode + resolved. Mode 2b visibility restored.

writers_inventory.current_schema: Emits `resolved: false`, no `status`. THIS PATCH SITE.
writers_inventory.impact_of_this_patch: Patch 2 applied. Post-patch: status + target_mode + resolved. Mode 2b visibility restored.

writers_inventory.current_schema: Documentation only. Declares `status: 'pending' | 'resolved'` as the canonical required field. Does NOT yet enumerate `target_mode` as an optional advisory field.
writers_inventory.impact_of_this_patch: NO CODE-PATH IMPACT (documentation, not a writer). Optional follow-up: amend DATA-SCHEMAS.md to add `target_mode: 'analyst-mode-1b' | 'tinker' | null` to the canonical schema. Listed in PROP-046.phase_2_optional_followup as out-of-scope; documentation can lag the writer change without breaking anything.

writers_inventory.current_schema: References attention-inbox.json by name only (no writer logic).
Mentions attention-inbox.json as a smart_copy target.

check_b_no_conflict_with_other_inbox_wri.verdict: NO CONFLICTS. Two existing writers (this patch's targets) are corrected to schema-conform. The one already-conforming writer (decider.md Step A) emits a slightly narrower shape (lacks the advisory target_mode field), but this is non-breaking because target_mode is advisory-only — no consumer branches on it.

check_c_existing_6_stale_assess_items_un.question: Confirm the existing 6 STALE-ASSESS items (operator-direct-fixed in commit 3688bbe with schema_fix_at field) won't be re-touched by this patch.
check_c_existing_6_stale_assess_items_un.current_state_of_those_items_pasted: From monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json (live snapshot 2026-05-17T20:55Z): 2 STALE-ASSESS items remain in the live file (STALE-ASSESS-PRED-106-2026-05-17 and STALE-ASSESS-PRED-NEW-002-2026-05-17), both carry `status: 'pending'`, `resolved: false`, `schema_fix_at: '2026-05-17T17:50:00Z'`. (The original 6 noted in the directive — the other 4 have been resolved/archived between commit 3688bbe and now per normal Mode 2b drain, e.g., 4 items moved to attention-inbox-archive.jsonl.)
check_c_existing_6_stale_assess_items_un.patch_target_is_the_prompt_not_the_data: Both patches edit MARKDOWN PROMPT FILES (.md), not the JSON state file (monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json). Editing a markdown prompt cannot in any way mutate items already sitting in the JSON file. The patches only affect how the writer-template is rendered the NEXT time decider's Step 1a fires (or analyst's Orphaned-Issues path fires); existing items pass through entirely untouched.
check_c_existing_6_stale_assess_items_un.what_about_the_target_mode_field_on_exis: The existing STALE-ASSESS items do NOT carry a `target_mode` field. This is by design — operator's commit 3688bbe retrofitted only the missing `status: 'pending'` field (the minimum needed to restore Mode 2b dispatcher visibility); it did not pre-add the advisory `target_mode` because Q1 had not yet been decided. Now that Q1 is decided (target_mode = canonical field), the operator can OPTIONALLY backfill `target_mode: 'analyst-mode-1b'` onto the 2 remaining live STALE-ASSESS items for shape-symmetry with future emits — but this is NOT part of this PROP's scope. Per directive out_of_scope_for_tinker[2]: 'Do NOT expand scope to other inbox writers beyond the two named sites'. Retrofitting existing items is a separate operator-discretion action.
check_c_existing_6_stale_assess_items_un.verdict: EXISTING ITEMS UNTOUCHED. The patches modify writer templates, not state files. The 2 remaining live STALE-ASSESS items (and any archived ones) keep their current shape unchanged.

regression_check.summary_one_paragraph: All three regression-check questions return clean. (a) Mode 2b dispatcher's `status === 'pending'` filter currently misses both writers' items because they lack the status field; after patches, both will emit status:'pending' and become visible — this is the desired fix, not a disruption. The added target_mode field is advisory-only and no current consumer reads it. (b) Three attention-inbox writers exist across the prompt set; one (decider.md Step A) is already schema-conforming and emits a narrower shape (no target_mode), which is non-breaking. The other two are this patch's targets and become schema-conforming after application. No conflicts. (c) The patches modify markdown prompt files only; the live JSON state file is unaffected. The 2 remaining live STALE-ASSESS items keep their current shape (status:'pending' + resolved:false + schema_fix_at), and backfilling target_mode onto them is out-of-scope per directive.

application_procedure.step_1_apply_patch_1: In the clean clone (/tmp/edit-clone or operator's working clone), use the Edit tool with file=monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md, old_string=patches[0].old_string, new_string=patches[0].new_string. Verify with `grep -n 'target_mode' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md` — should return exactly one line containing `target_mode: 'analyst-mode-1b',` within ~5 lines of `created_by: 'decider-stale-skip'`.
application_procedure.step_2_apply_patch_2: In the same clone, use the Edit tool with file=monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode1-expansions.md, old_string=patches[1].old_string, new_string=patches[1].new_string. Verify with `grep -n 'target_mode' monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode1-expansions.md` — should return exactly one line containing `"target_mode": "tinker"` within ~5 lines of `PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED`.
application_procedure.step_3_run_tests: Run `node test.js 2>&1 | tail -20` to confirm no regression. (These are prompt-text edits, so tests should be unaffected; this is belt-and-suspenders.)
application_procedure.step_4_commit_and_push: Single commit with message like `PROP-046 Phase 1 — fix attention-inbox writer schema in two prompts (status:pending + target_mode:<lane>)`. Push from the clone.
application_procedure.step_5_promote_prop046_status: Optionally, update PROP-046.status from 'design-pending-operator-review' to 'integrated' with `integrated_at` field. Operator discretion.
application_procedure.step_6_mark_directive_completed: Tinker (this agent) marks DIRECTIVE-20260517-004 status='completed' with completed_at + completed_by_run + prop_id_authored fields (per CLAUDE.md DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception).

verification_pattern.description: How to verify this amendment landed correctly. Tinker should run this on the next post-application run to confirm FIXED.

check_1_writer_decider_intake_target_mod.expected: Exactly one line containing `target_mode: 'analyst-mode-1b',` near the stale-skip writer (within ~5 lines of `created_by: 'decider-stale-skip'`).

check_2_writer_analyst_mode1_target_mode.expected: Exactly one line containing `"target_mode": "tinker"` near the orphan-detected JSON example (within ~5 lines of `PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED`).
check_2_writer_analyst_mode1_target_mode.failure_mode: Empty grep result → patch 2 not applied. Multiple matches → unintended duplicate insertion.

check_3_writer_decider_intake_status_pen.expected: 1 (the status:'pending' field is now adjacent to the stale-skip writer).
check_3_writer_decider_intake_status_pen.failure_mode: 0 → patch 1 did not insert the status field. >1 → unintended duplicate.

check_4_writer_analyst_mode1_status_pend.expected: 1 (the status:'pending' field is now adjacent to the orphan-detected example).
check_4_writer_analyst_mode1_status_pend.failure_mode: 0 → patch 2 did not insert the status field. >1 → unintended duplicate.

check_5_next_emit_conforms_at_runtime.command: After next decider-stale-skip OR PROP029-orphan fire, inspect the new attention-inbox item: `node -e "const a=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json','utf8'));const arr=Array.isArray(a)?a:(a.items||[]);const newest=arr[arr.length-1];console.log(newest.id, newest.status, newest.target_mode, newest.resolved)"`
check_5_next_emit_conforms_at_runtime.expected: newest.status === 'pending' AND newest.target_mode in ['analyst-mode-1b','tinker'] AND newest.resolved === false.
check_5_next_emit_conforms_at_runtime.failure_mode: Either field missing or empty → the prompt edit was string-matched wrong or there is a SECOND writer path not patched.

out_of_scope_followups_listed_for_awaren: Amend DATA-SCHEMAS.md § analyst/attention-inbox.json to declare `target_mode: 'analyst-mode-1b' | 'tinker' | 'decider-intake' | null` as an optional advisory field (per PROP-046.phase_2_optional_followup[0]).

Backfill `target_mode: 'analyst-mode-1b'` onto the 2 remaining live STALE-ASSESS items in monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json for shape-symmetry with future emits (operator discretion; out-of-scope per directive guardrail).

Optionally add `target_mode: '<lane>'` to the third attention-inbox writer (monitor/prompts/decider.md Step A End-of-Run inbox writer) for full shape-symmetry across all three writers (PROP-046's body chose to leave this writer alone since it already emits status:'pending'; symmetry-cleanup PROP could be filed later).

If the universal-orphan-batch from N=137 surfaces a recurring tinker workload, file a separate PROP to introduce a tinker sub-mode keyed off `target_mode: 'tinker-mode-<N>'` (per Q2 rationale: current N=137 spike is one-time, not a class).
